<insights>

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
- The suite covers at least 13 command scenarios (adapter, analyze, import, prune, quiet, recommend, smart-add, sync, and more), providing broad regression protection for the entire public CLI interface.
- Zero coupling to internal zones confirms these tests are true black-box — they interact only through the CLI boundary, making them resilient to internal refactors.
- Perfect cohesion (1.0) means the e2e tests are self-contained and can run in isolation in CI without shared mutable state with unit or integration layers.
- All 13+ files are pure test files, satisfying the '-tests' naming convention; the zone correctly has no production source entrypoints.
- The breadth of CLI commands covered suggests e2e tests are the primary safety net for the user-facing interface — ensure they run on every PR to prevent CLI regressions from shipping.
- e2e-tests zone covers no Notion-specific CLI surface despite notion-store-client being an active adapter — any CLI flags or behaviors unique to the Notion integration are untested at the black-box level.
- e2e-tests zone is the only zone with both perfect cohesion AND zero coupling simultaneously with notion-store-client — both are structurally exemplary but their lack of connection represents a gap in end-to-end adapter coverage.
- e2e-tests has 13 files covering 13+ CLI commands but the suite appears to have no shared test fixtures or helpers within the zone — perfect cohesion with no visible utility files suggests either all setup is inline (increasing duplication risk) or helpers live outside the zone boundary.
- The apparent 1:1 mapping between e2e test files and CLI commands (cli-adapter, cli-analyze, cli-import, etc.) is an implicit convention with no enforcement mechanism — a new CLI command added without a matching e2e file in this zone will not trigger any lint, build, or test failure.
- With 13 test files and no visible shared helper or fixture files inside the zone boundary, either all setup logic is inlined per-file (increasing duplication as the suite grows) or shared helpers live outside the zone in a location that the import graph would register as a cross-zone edge — the absence of any coupling edge means all setup is currently inlined or uses only Node/test-framework globals.
- Introduce a lint rule or CI check that verifies every file in src/cli/commands/ has a corresponding tests/e2e/cli-<command>.test.ts file. The current implicit 1:1 convention is unenforced and will silently break as the CLI surface grows.
- Audit each of the 13 e2e test files for duplicated setup patterns (process spawning, temp directory creation, fixture loading). If duplication exists, extract a shared e2e test helper into the zone (e.g. tests/e2e/helpers/cli-runner.ts) to reduce maintenance surface without breaking the zero-coupling property — internal helpers do not add cross-zone edges.
- [call graph] 238 internal calls, 0 outgoing, 0 incoming (cohesion: 1, coupling: 0)
